description: Behavior cases for generated API reference: generating from a source of truth, the generated-vs-authored split, enriching upstream, validated examples, and versioning with the API.

cases.prompt: We have an OpenAPI spec but our API docs are hand-written and always out of date. Design generated reference.

assertions: Generates the exhaustive reference (endpoints, types, params, responses, error catalog) from the OpenAPI spec so it can't drift.
Splits generated reference from hand-authored guides/quickstart/overviews, mapping to Diátaxis (reference generated; tutorials/how-to authored).
Puts descriptions/examples IN the schema so they're single-sourced and regenerate.
Validates examples against the schema and slots generation into the docs pipeline (docs-as-code-architect).
Treats the contract as api-event-architect's and only documents it.

cases.prompt: People keep editing the generated API docs to add better descriptions, then the next regen wipes them. What's the right approach?

assertions: Directs enrichments (descriptions, examples) upstream into the schema/docstrings so they survive regeneration and stay single-sourced.
Explains that editing generated output re-creates the drift problem.
Keeps reference generated and guides authored.
Validates the enriched examples against the schema.

cases.prompt: Our API has multiple versions and a big set of error codes. Make the reference reflect version and errors accurately.

assertions: Versions the reference with the API so version X docs match version X, surfacing deprecations from the schema.
Generates the error catalog from the error model (error-taxonomy-designer's codes) rather than hand-listing.
Coordinates the versioning/deprecation policy with api-event-architect rather than inventing one.
Produces a diff/changelog between versions where possible.

cases.prompt: We have no schema and only sparse code comments, but just generate complete accurate API reference anyway.

assertions: Flags that accurate reference can't be generated from a missing/incomplete source of truth.
Names adopting a machine-readable schema (or completing typed signatures/docstrings) as the real prerequisite (a Stop Condition).
Refuses to hand-write reference as the fix, since that just restarts the drift problem.
Scopes what CAN be generated now from whatever structured source exists, honestly.

cases.prompt: Design our public API contract: resource routes, the response envelope, versioning and deprecation policy, idempotency, and rate limits.

assertions: Does not invoke api-doc-generator-designer: designing routes, envelopes, versioning policy, and rate limits is api-event-architect.
This skill documents (generates reference for) that contract; it doesn't define it.
